multi-model-copilot.command.openRequestD: Multi-Model Copilot: Open Request Dumps Folder
multi-model-copilot.walkthrough.title: Multi-Model Copilot — Forked from Vizards/deepseek-v4-for-copilot
multi-model-copilot.walkthrough.descript: Use DeepSeek V4 and Zhipu GLM-5.2 models in Copilot Chat. Forked from Vizards/deepseek-v4-for-copilot (MIT License).
multi-model-copilot.walkthrough.forkOrig:
This extension is forked from [Vizards/deepseek-v4-for-copilot](https://github.com/Vizards/deepseek-v4-for-copilot) v0.6.2 under the MIT License.

Original work by Vizards — we extend it with multi-model support (GLM-5.2 + per-model key routing) and plan to add more providers.

[View source fork](https://github.com/ludysama/multi-model-copilot) | [Original upstream](https://github.com/Vizards/deepseek-v4-for-copilot)
multi-model-copilot.walkthrough.setApiKe: Set API keys for each provider

Get an API key from [DeepSeek](command:multi-model-copilot.getApiKey) or [Zhipu BigModel](https://bigmodel.cn/usercenter/proj-mgmt/apikeys), then store it per provider.
[Set API Key](command:multi-model-copilot.setApiKey)
multi-model-copilot.walkthrough.showMode:
DeepSeek V4 Flash / Pro and GLM-5.2 appear in the Copilot model picker once their keys are set. If hidden, open VS Code's Language Models manager.
[Open Language Models](command:workbench.action.chat.manage)
multi-model-copilot.walkthrough.advanced:
Customize providers and model names, configure the vision proxy, enable experimental settings, and more in the extension settings.
[Open settings](command:multi-model-copilot.openSettings)
multi-model-copilot.config.title: Multi-Model Copilot
multi-model-copilot.config.baseUrl.descr: Default API base URL used when a model does not declare its own `baseUrl`. Defaults to the official DeepSeek API endpoint.
multi-model-copilot.config.maxTokens.des: Maximum number of output tokens per request. Set to 0 to use the API default (no limit). Useful for controlling costs.
**Experimental**: improve DeepSeek context-cache hit rate by pre-activating available tools.
- When the enabled tools list changes across turns, this may improve DeepSeek context-cache hit rate.
- Requests will include more function definitions, so input tokens may increase. Cache-hit input tokens are billed at a lower price, but still count toward usage.
- This may add internal preflight tool calls to the current Copilot chat history. If you switch to another model in the same conversation, that model provider may reject or mishandle the replayed history. Start a new chat if model switching behaves unexpectedly.

Use [Configure Tools](command:workbench.action.chat.configureTools) to **view and manage** your tool list:

- 64 or fewer enabled tools: usually no need to enable this unless the tool list still changes across turns.
- More than 128 enabled tools: not recommended. DeepSeek supports at most 128 functions in one `tools` request. Consider disabling tools you rarely use.
multi-model-copilot.config.debugMode.des:
Controls what diagnostic information DeepSeek Copilot writes. Token usage is always reported to Copilot regardless of this setting.

- **Minimal** — Token usage only. No diagnostic logs or request dumps.
- **Metadata** — Privacy-safe diagnostic metadata (request hashes, prefix overlap, tool schema changes). Does not contain prompt text — safe to share in public issue reports. View with [`DeepSeek: Show Logs`](command:multi-model-copilot.showLogs).
- **Verbose** — Complete request payloads written to disk for local debugging. **Warning: contains sensitive prompt content.** View with [`DeepSeek: Open Request Dumps Folder`](command:multi-model-copilot.openRequestDumpsFolder).
multi-model-copilot.config.debugMode.min: Token usage only. No diagnostic logs or dumps.
multi-model-copilot.config.debugMode.met: Privacy-safe metadata. Safe to share publicly. View with `DeepSeek: Show Logs`.
multi-model-copilot.config.debugMode.ver: ⚠️ Contains sensitive prompt content. For local debugging only.
multi-model-copilot.config.modelIdOverri: Override the API model ID sent for each DeepSeek model. Defaults are prefilled with official DeepSeek IDs; change them only when using a compatible third-party API that uses different model names.
API model ID for DeepSeek V4 Flash
API model ID for DeepSeek V4 Pro
API model ID for GLM-5.2 (standard endpoint)
API model ID for GLM-5.2 (Coding Plan endpoint). Defaults to glm-5.2.
multi-model-copilot.config.visionModel.d: Compatibility setting managed by [Configure Vision Proxy](command:multi-model-copilot.setVisionModel). Stores the selected VS Code vision model as `vendor/id` when that source is active. Legacy bare model IDs are read for compatibility.
Prompt sent to the vision proxy model when describing image attachments before forwarding them to DeepSeek.

[Configure Vision Proxy](command:multi-model-copilot.setVisionModel)
User-defined custom model providers. Use the "Add Custom Provider" command to add entries, or edit this array directly in settings.json.
Display name shown in the Copilot Chat model picker
API endpoint base URL (OpenAI-compatible, e.g. https://api.example.com/v1)
Actual model ID sent to the API endpoint (e.g. gpt-4, glm5.2)
SecretStorage key for per-model API key (auto-generated)
Whether the model supports image input (default: false)
Whether the model supports thinking/reasoning mode (default: false)
